seasonality: XRP transaction volume is event-driven, spiking around Ripple legal developments (SEC lawsuit milestones), exchange listings, and macro crypto bull cycles rather than consistent seasonal patterns.
rationale: XRP is the native asset of the XRP Ledger (XRPL), a layer-1 payment-focused blockchain. Transaction fees on XRPL are burned (not distributed to token holders or a protocol treasury), averaging roughly 0.00001 XRP per transaction; with ~1–2 million daily transactions the annualized fee burn is on the order of a few thousand USD equivalent — economically negligible and not 'revenue' in any conventional protocol sense. DefiLlama does list XRPL TVL (approximately $50–80M in on-chain DEX/AMM liquidity as of mid-2025), but Ripple the company's revenue (from XRP sales and ODL corridors, reported at ~$285M in Q1 2024 per Ripple's own disclosures) accrues to a private corporation, not to XRP token holders, making P/S and yield metrics inapplicable to the token itself. Because there is no staking mechanism, no fee distribution to holders, and no on-chain treasury, real yield and inflationary yield are both null.
